trade.summary: Trade volume is moderate given the geographic distance. Australia exports coal, beef, and aluminium to Canada, while Canada exports aircraft, machinery, and processed goods. Both economies have similar resource-export profiles that limit complementarity. An FTA framework under CPTPP supports trade.

diplomatic.summary: Australia and Canada share deep ties as Commonwealth nations, Five Eyes partners, and like-minded Western democracies. Regular high-level consultations occur on defence, intelligence, trade, and multilateral policy. Both nations closely coordinate positions at the UN, G20, and other multilateral forums.

military.summary: Strong military-to-military relationship through Five Eyes intelligence sharing, NATO partnerships, and bilateral defence cooperation. Both countries have deployed together in Afghanistan, Iraq, and other multilateral missions. Regular interoperability exercises.

financial.summary: Significant cross-investment exists, particularly in mining and resources. Canadian pension funds have substantial Australian infrastructure assets. Australian mining companies operate in Canada. Shared legal frameworks ease investment flows.

energy.summary: Minimal direct energy trade given geographic distance. Both are major energy exporters. Some cooperation on LNG technology and regulatory frameworks.

scientific.summary: Extensive research collaboration across universities and government agencies. Joint programs in climate science, agriculture, health, and AI. Strong academic exchange facilitated by shared language and compatible research cultures.
